raw_consumer_statement: I have had a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( and other XXXX XXXX accounts ) for years and have been paying off balances every month with automatic payments of {$500.00} or more. I recently noticed fraudulent charges from XXXX 2025 on my account and contacted the company on I believe XX/XX/year> after checking my balance after the automatic payment went through. I noticed my account balance was going up so I checked transaction history. I usually just check balance. I contacted them and disputed XXXX transactions totaling {$1700.00}. They placed a conditional credit on the account at that time. Today, XX/XX/year>, I checked again and noticed the charges were again charged back to my account. When I called they claimed their investigation only consisted of checking my log in history and since I logged into my account ( probably just checking my balance ) during the time the charges were made that I am now responsible. They even had some of the wrong charges disputed. When I tried to explain it was only XXXX and XXXX charges that werent me, she just dismissed me. That is ridiculous. I pay by bills and for what I charge but do not want to pay for fraudulent charges. When I asked if they checked the cameras on the registers for the date and time of the fraudulent charges they said it was my responsibility to file a police report and have the police pull the camera footage. XXXX has much bigger things to deal with and will not waste that kind of resources on my {$1700.00} issue. XXXX XXXX could pull that footage easily and prove that it wasnt me making those charges but they refuse. I dont know what else to do. I have impeccable credit and always pay my bills but do not want to be responsible for charges I did not make. I hope you can help me resolve this issue.
